Luna Loves London
Discover Story Centre
Sat 25 Jul, 10:05
2-5 / family
Book

An immersive exhibition for families. Join Luna and friends for a very special trip to a very big city. This isn’t the London of Big Ben and the changing of the guard though. This is Luna’s London – full of real city life. There are all the things you’d expect from the city: a library, a community garden, a place to practice sport and a dance studio complete with a disco ball. You can visit the art gallery (and have a go at making some art yourself), or dress up and take part in the local street carnival! ------ Luna Loves London is an immersive exhibition full of storytelling, play and imagination. It has been made by Discover with the dream-team of Joseph Coelho and Fiona Lumbers – the creators of the acclaimed Luna Loves… book series.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)

Julián Is a Mermaid BSL Interpreted
Discover Story Centre
Sat 22 Aug, 14:35
2-5 / family
Book

What if you could become exactly who you imagine? A storytelling adventure for children and their grown-ups Step into a world of colour, imagination, and self-expression with our beautiful storytelling adaptation of Julián Is a Mermaid, inspired by Jessica Love’s celebrated picture book. In this 20-minute immersive storytelling piece, children and their adults will be swept into a world of mermaids, imagination, and joyful self-expression. Then stay for 10 minutes of playful family dress up and dancing. A magical celebration of individuality, acceptance, and the freedom to dream.BSL Interpreted edition

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Luna Loves London BSL Interpreted
Discover Story Centre
Sat 22 Aug, 16:10
2-5 / family
Book

An immersive exhibition for families. Join Luna and friends for a very special trip to a very big city. This isn’t the London of Big Ben and the changing of the guard though. This is Luna’s London – full of real city life. There are all the things you’d expect from the city: a library, a community garden, a place to practice sport and a dance studio complete with a disco ball. You can visit the art gallery (and have a go at making some art yourself), or dress up and take part in the local street carnival! ------ Luna Loves London is an immersive exhibition full of storytelling, play and imagination. 383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Luna Loves London-Sensory Adapted
Discover Story Centre
Sun 23 Aug, 16:10
2-5 / family
Book

At Discover, our events and performances are all ‘relaxed’ so that all families can enjoy them – we don’t expect our young audiences to be completely still or quiet! Our new ‘Sensory Adapted’ sessions are modified in various ways, to make them more suitable for certain families with SEN. Some of the differences are: We will explicitly indicate that our audience is welcome to move around, make noise and leave or re-enter the session throughout. We’ve kept capacity lower, and are holding these sessions at times when Discover is generally quieter throughout the building. Lights and sound levels will be adapted to make these sessions less overwhelming – we’ll also have a specific chillout area outside for those who need a break. Ear defenders will be offered to those who need them (NB these are always available on request at our reception).

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Imagine, Make, Do – Crafting with Christina Rose Brown
Discover Story Centre
Mon 24 Aug, 11:00
2-5 / family
Book

Get ready to imagine, make, and do! In these fun family workshops, you’ll be using Lego in surprising new ways — including printing with it to create your own artworks. Team up with your grownup and let your creativity run wild. Led by artist educator Christina Rose Brown.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Dylan, Daisy, and the Disco Ball Draw-along
Discover Story Centre
Fri 28 Aug, 11:00
2-5 / family
Book

Join award-winning author Robert Tregoning for a joyful interactive story time and draw-along inspired by the latest release from Robert and illustrator Laura Brenlla, Dylan, Daisy, and the Disco Ball. Dive into a vibrant new story, from the creators of The Rock Family Band, all about self-confidence, self-expression, and finding your own path. With plenty of opportunities to join in, get creative, and draw along, this lively session is perfect for children who love stories, music, and a chance to shine in their own unique way.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)

Bankside / Millbank, London Check venue ~30 mins Source: Tate
Unicorn Theatre
Toto Kerblammo!
Fri 08 May, 19:00
2-5 / family
Book

For ages 9-13/ School years' 5-8 / Run time: 70 mins (no interval) / Written and directed by Tim Crouch See with your ears. Poor Effy. Sent to live with her aunt and uncle in a block that won’t allow dogs. What’s a girl to do? She smuggles her furry friend in, of course. Keeps him under a blanket. Feeds him biscuits. Ssh Toto, quiet. But it can’t last forever. Effy needs saving. And Toto is up to the task. Tim Crouch’s immersive new play is a tender and powerful story about listening, friendship and finding hope in the darkest of places. Wearing headphones, the audience will experience Effy’s world through 3D binaural audio technology. Immerse yourself in a compelling live mix of sound and action, and discover the sound of things you never knew had sounds… ‘One of the most important writers and theatre makers in the UK’ (The Stage), Tim Crouch (Beginners) returns to the Unicorn with his signature blend of thought-provoking and experimental theatre.

147 Tooley St, London SE1 2HZ Check venue ~35 mins Source: Spektrix (unicorntheatre)
Planet Omar
Thu 21 May, 11:00
2-5 / family
Book

For ages 7+ / Welcome to the imaginative brain of Omar! Step into the fantastically imaginative and chaotic world of Planet Omar, following the adventures of an 8-year-old with a wild imagination, a big heart, and a knack for getting into trouble. This vibrant new stage adaptation based on the beloved books by Zanib Mian has been brought to life by award-winning playwright Asif Khan and director Sameena Hussain. When Omar’s family moves to a new neighbourhood, he faces a whirlwind of challenges: a new school, an unfriendly neighbour, and a bully who makes every day feel like a battle. But with courage, kindness, and a little help from his family and new best friend Charlie, Omar discovers his not-so-average imagination is his greatest strength. This world premiere production is a joyful and funny celebration of empathy, identity, and resilience. With dynamic storytelling, playful visuals, and a heartwarming message, Planet Omar invites audiences of all ages to laugh, reflect, and cheer for a hero who dares to dream big, even when the odds are stacked against him. Perfect for families and school groups, this adaptation is a powerful reminder that imagination can be a superpower, and that friendship and understanding can change the world. Winner of The Little Rebels Award Nominated for the 2019 Carnegie Medal Longlisted for the 2019 UKLA AWARD A Production by Leeds Playhouse, in association with the Unicorn Theatre and Birmingham Repertory Theatre With special thanks to Charles Holloway OBE, Weston Theatre Season Partner.

147 Tooley St, London SE1 2HZ Check venue ~35 mins Source: Spektrix (unicorntheatre)
Whatever the Weather
Fri 03 Jul, 10:00
2-5 / family
Book

Run-time: 45 mins/ Ages 0-7 Whatever the Weather is an immersive playscape experience inviting children and their grown-ups to play creatively together. Get closer to nature and experience rain, snow, sunshine, and more, in this playful, interactive space designed to uncover the wonders of weather and how it shapes our everyday lives. Inspired by ideas from local children, Whatever the Weather is an artist-designed space that celebrates the different ways children naturally play - encouraging families to discover, imagine and explore together. A Unicorn Theatre and Theatre Hullabaloo production.

147 Tooley St, London SE1 2HZ Check venue ~35 mins Source: Spektrix (unicorntheatre)
The Lion Inside
Tue 14 Jul, 13:30
2-5 / family
Book

A heart-warming story about confidence, self-esteem, and a shy little mouse who sets out on a journey to find his roar. ‘In a dry dusty place where the sand sparkled gold, stood a mighty flat rock, all craggy and old. And under that rock in a tinyful house, lived the littlest, quietest, meekest brown mouse.’ Fed up of being ignored and forgotten by the other animals, Mouse wishes he could roar like Lion. But, as he discovers, even the biggest, bossiest people are scared sometimes ... and even the smallest creatures can have the heart of a lion! Based on the multi-million copy selling book by Rachel Bright & Jim Field published by Hachette Children’s Group, this brand-new adaptation for stage is directed by Sarah Punshon (The Jungle Book), with music and lyrics by Eamonn O’Dwyer (Brief Encounter).

147 Tooley St, London SE1 2HZ Check venue ~35 mins Source: Spektrix (unicorntheatre)

2-5 / family
Book

Join a Daniel Wallis to create your very own magical story cubes! Using recycled materials, we’ll build and transform ordinary boxes into tiny treasure chests filled with imagination. Each cube will hold a surprise theme: place, activity, object, or character, helping you spin wild adventures, invent new worlds, and bring your stories to life!

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
The Dreamies: A Story & Dream Catcher Workshop
Wed 27 May, 11:00
2-5 / family
Book

Join author Iona Campbell for a magical storytelling adventure with her beautiful book The Dreamies. Settle in for a dreamy storytime full of imagination as we journey through the world of The Dreamies. Then it’s time to get creative! Using colourful materials, you’ll design and make your very own dream catcher to take home.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)

Book

Our Out of the Box season celebrates imagination and transformation, turning everyday recycled materials into playful worlds, characters and stories. As we reopen our new building, this season is all about creativity, joy and big ideas made from simple things.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Create Your Own Hero or Monster!
Fri 29 May, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

Join Daniel Wallis to bring your imagination to life as you create your very own larger-than-life character! Draw around your body and transform your outline into a superhero or silly monster using colour, collage, and creative materials. Then comes the fun twist, swap pieces with other creators to invent a brand-new mixed-up character! Add speech bubbles to give your character a voice and a personality, and watch your wild new hero or monster come to life.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Don’t Intrude on my Mood! with author Rea Malhotram
Mon 01 Jun, 16:00
2-5 / family
Book

Event Blurb: A great way to teach young readers the importance of patience and self-reflection, this story is written for those who often take a trip to Grumpsville, and need a little help with getting back. This interactive session will help children describe and demonstrate appropriate ways to cope with challenging emotions. Book blurb: Don’t Intrude on my Mood! is about a little cat who throws a big tantrum! As a result of his fiery temper, he rants and raves and rants some more- only to be interrupted by his mother, who has some unexpected news... Facilitator bio: Rea is an award-winning children’s author, who is passionate about telling stories full of heart and humour.   Born and brought up in Bombay with a voracious appetite for company, Rea adores books and food in equal measure. Like most grown-ups, she juggles many responsibilities, such as being an English and Drama teacher, a freelance writer, actor and voice-over artist.   When she isn’t busy being responsible, Rea enjoys swimming with jellyfish, dancing in muddy fields, and buying far too many sparkly things.   Her stories are full of funky hairdos, grumpy kittens, and Spanish soap dishes. It’s hard work being this silly, but someone’s got to do it. Socials: @rea_mm

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Day Entry
Sat 20 Jun, 10:00
2-5 / family
Book

Attend anytime throughout the day, Open everyday from 10:00am-17:00pm with access to Story Worlds and Story Garden

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Baby and Toddler Sensory Story Space
Sat 20 Jun, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

A beautiful, enchanted garden-themed play room for our very smallest visitors. For 0-2 year olds and their grown-ups. This brand-new sensory space has been created with the help of the University of East London’s Institute for the Science of Early Years and Youth, as a dedicated area for 0-2 year-olds and their grown-ups. Featuring soft surfaces (and shoes off!), lights, textures, a windy tunnel, curated books and even a baby waterfall – this is a corner of Discover made just for littler ones. This room provides a calm space to read and physically engage with books, stories and sensory and imaginative play.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Meet the Author: Dapo Adeola
Sat 20 Jun, 11:30
2-5 / family
Book

Fly into an empowering event with bestselling author and award-winning illustrator Dapo Adeola as he introduces his latest picture book Super Goat Girl. Find out what makes you super in this funny, heartwarming event full of drawing and finding joy in who you are.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Not-A-Box
Sat 20 Jun, 11:35
2-5 / family
Book

A box is just a box… or is it? Join us for a delightful storytelling experience inspired by Not a Box by Antoinette Portis, created especially for children aged 0–5 and their grown-ups. Through playful performance, gentle interaction, and imaginative storytelling, little audiences are invited to explore how the simplest object can become something extraordinary. From soaring rocket ships to towering mountains, follow a small rabbit on a big adventure that celebrates curiosity, creativity, and the power of saying “what if?” This enchanting show encourages children to see the world not just as it is, but as it could be.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Meet the Author: Draw with Rob Biddulph
Sun 21 Jun, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

Join bestselling and multi award-winning author and illustrator Rob Biddulph, the creative star behind the viral and phenomenal #DrawWithRob videos for a fun packed event for all the family. As part of this event Rob will bring to life his award-winning books, including his latest picture book When I Grow Up I'd Like to Be..., and everyone will learn how to draw one of Rob’s amazing illustrations so please do come with some paper, or a sketch book and pencils/pens. No materials required, we’ll supply the paper and pencils!

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Baby Sing and Sign
Mon 22 Jun, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

Join Sing and Sign for a summer themed 30 minutes of singing, music and fun. We’ll teach you some signs that you can use at home every day alongside some seasonal signs to use during those sunny days spent outside exploring. Using baby sign has been proven to encourage speech, reduce frustration and increase communication skills, allowing your little one to tell you their wants and needs and what excites them in the world around them. Bio: Rachel has been running Sing and Sign classes in Newham for 5 years and is a local resident too with two young daughters, both of them used baby sign while they were younger and they both love to visit Discover. 

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
The Laugh Lab Talk with Nikki Jecks
Mon 22 Jun, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

Babies are funnier than we give them credit for. Long before they say their first word or take their first step, infants are already doing something quietly extraordinary: trying to make people laugh. They pull toys away just as you reach for them. They put things on their heads. They make faces they know will get a reaction, then watch your face to see if it worked. But infant humour is more than charming behaviour. It turns out that what makes a baby laugh – and what a baby does to make you laugh – is one of the most revealing windows we have into the developing brain. Each joke, each tease, each bit of deliberate silliness is evidence of something profound happening: the emergence of expectations, the beginnings of social understanding, and the earliest hints of a capacity that won't fully mature for years – the ability to model another person's mind. This talk explores what developmental science has discovered about humour in the first year of life: when it appears, how it changes, and why it matters far beyond the giggle.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
The Three Butterflies: A Sensory Storytelling Experience
Mon 22 Jun, 12:00
2-5 / family
Book

The Three Butterflies is a gentle sensory storytelling experience using puppets, music and interactive play. Inspired by a traditional German folk tale, this heartwarming story celebrates love, friendship and standing together in the face of discrimination. Makaton signs and sensory props are woven throughout the story, creating a welcoming and inclusive experience for all children. Before the storytelling begins, families are invited to explore the story setting, helping little ones feel comfortable and curious about the world they are about to enter.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
The Very Groovy Caterpillar: Puppet Making and Storytelling Workshop
Tue 23 Jun, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

A puppety, musical, crafty telling of a classic story. Come into the garden and make a simple puppet of a little caterpillar. then take it on a story journey with props and songs, finding smells and colours in the garden and of course lots to eat. Then watch as the caterpillar makes a cocoon and emerges as a beautiful butterfly and have a dance to celebrate.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Baby Sensory Circus
Wed 24 Jun, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

Join Story Builders Annalena and Bri for a fun and colourful 10-minute circus performance with Play Time afterwards. These two clowns are desperate for a holiday, but how are they going to get there? And what hilarious obstacles are they going to encounter on their way? Play with glowing juggling balls, colourful poi or funky hoola hoops! An exciting exploration of circus elements for the whole family!

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Tiny Travelling Band
Thu 25 Jun, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

Join Story Builders Annalena and Hannah for a participatory music session aimed towards 0–4-year-olds and their families. What does a viola sound like, a recorder, a trumpet? This travelling band knows all kinds of nursery rhymes, Disney repertoire and folk tunes. Come along, clap along, shake along, dance along! Experience tiny tunes.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Baby Storytelling: Hello Sun with Sharon King-Chai
Fri 26 Jun, 10:30
2-5 / family
Book

Join author and illustrator Sharon King-Chai for a joyful introduction to Hello Sun, her enchanting mirror book created especially for children under four. With gentle storytelling and circle time activities, little ones will explore first words, animals and weather with the help of colourful and interactive character props that spark curiosity. Warm, nurturing and full of wonder, this event offers families a delightful way to learn, play and connect through stories.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Shadow Creatures: A Nighttime Puppet Adventure
Fri 26 Jun, 13:00
2-5 / family
Book

Step into the night and discover a world of glowing shadows and nocturnal creatures! In this immersive craft experience, families will create their own shadow puppets inspired by nighttime animals. Using simple materials and easy-to-follow instructions, you’ll design your puppet before heading into our magical darkened space. Armed with torches, families can explore the “night,” casting their shadow puppets onto the walls and watching their creatures come to life. Perfect for younger visitors, the space will also include soft cushions, gentle lighting, and glowing sensory toys. Pre-made puppets will be available so even babies and very young children can join in the shadow play and exploration.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Wee Movers: Baby Dance Session
Sat 27 Jun, 11:30
2-5 / family
Book

Join Wee Movers for a storytelling and dance adventure designed especially for babies and their grown-ups. After an energetic warm-up to prepare our bodies and minds we will delve into a story and explore its themes through dance. All sessions will be led by Fiona Campbell, founder of Wee Movers. Wee Movers offers playful, energetic creative dance classes for all ages. Combining contemporary and creative dance, the classes offer a fun introduction to movement where children and adults are encouraged to explore and express themselves in a warm and supportive environment.

Meet the Author: Draw-a-long with Nick Sharratt
Sat 11 Jul, 11:00
2-5 / family
Book

Join children’s author and illustrator Nick Sharratt for this utterly hair-larious event! Nick will be sharing his brand-new picture book, Mullets and taking a look at the hairstyles of characters in other favourite picture books like Shark in the Park and You Choose. There’ll be drawing, laughter and comb-pletely terrible puns! Family event for 3+.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Luna Loves London
Sat 25 Jul, 10:05
2-5 / family
Book

An immersive exhibition for families. Join Luna and friends for a very special trip to a very big city. This isn’t the London of Big Ben and the changing of the guard though. This is Luna’s London – full of real city life. There are all the things you’d expect from the city: a library, a community garden, a place to practice sport and a dance studio complete with a disco ball. You can visit the art gallery (and have a go at making some art yourself), or dress up and take part in the local street carnival! ------ Luna Loves London is an immersive exhibition full of storytelling, play and imagination. It has been made by Discover with the dream-team of Joseph Coelho and Fiona Lumbers – the creators of the acclaimed Luna Loves… book series.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Julián Is a Mermaid
Sun 02 Aug, 11:35
2-5 / family
Book

Event Blurb: What if you could become exactly who you imagine? A storytelling adventure for children and their grown-ups Step into a world of colour, imagination, and self-expression with our beautiful storytelling adaptation of Julián Is a Mermaid, inspired by Jessica Love’s celebrated picture book. In this 20-minute immersive storytelling piece, children and their adults will be swept into a world of mermaids, imagination, and joyful self-expression. Then stay for 10 minutes of playful family dress up and dancing. A magical celebration of individuality, acceptance, and the freedom to dream.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Imagine, Make, Do – Crafting with Anna Saunders
Mon 03 Aug, 11:00
2-5 / family
Book

Get ready to imagine, make, and do! In these fun family workshops, you’ll be using Lego in surprising new ways — including printing with it to create your own artworks. Team up with your grownup and let your creativity run wild. Led by artist educator Anna Saunders.

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Lego & STEM with UnPlugged Tots
Fri 07 Aug, 11:00
2-5 / family
Book

Unplugged Tots will be at the Discover Children’s Story Centre with activities that invite you to slow down, play with purpose, and reconnect with each other. We will be playing, crafting, moving, possibly getting a little messy but having a lot of fun (whilst learning at the same time!)    Examples of things they will do:    Algorithms with Cloud Dough & googly eyes   Building Lego Mazes   Sequencing with Threading   Landmarks Navigation and Maps using chalks   I’m Hannah Hagon, founder of Unplugged Tots, a movement that began with one simple belief: play matters. I help families step away from the noise and rediscover connection through the giggles, mess, and magic of screen-free play. For me, play isn’t just something children do, it’s how they make sense of the world, how they build resilience, and how they learn to think deeply and creatively. My work weaves in early computational thinking skills like pattern recognition and problem-solving, but always in a way that feels accessible, kind, and led by curiosity, not pressure.    https://www.linkedin.com/company/unplugged-tots  

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Den Building on Story Street

2-5 / family
Book

Come and build your Castle of Dreams, Secret Spy Hideaway or Creature’s Lair using our den‑making kits, then bring it to life through storytelling with Story‑and Den‑Builder extraordinaire Arkem! Bio: Arkem has created stories from the collection at YoungV&A, brought Drag Pirates to the National Maritime Museum and built sets for Hackney Shed Children's Theatre Company.

Luna Loves London BSL Interpreted
Sat 22 Aug, 16:10

An immersive exhibition for families. Join Luna and friends for a very special trip to a very big city. This isn’t the London of Big Ben and the changing of the guard though. There are all the things you’d expect from the city: a library, a community garden, a place to practice sport and a dance studio complete with a disco ball. 383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)
Luna Loves London-Sensory Adapted
Sun 23 Aug, 16:10
2-5 / family
Book

At Discover, our events and performances are all ‘relaxed’ so that all families can enjoy them – we don’t expect our young audiences to be completely still or quiet! Our new ‘Sensory Adapted’ sessions are modified in various ways, to make them more suitable for certain families with SEN. Some of the differences are: We will explicitly indicate that our audience is welcome to move around, make noise and leave or re-enter the session throughout. We’ve kept capacity lower, and are holding these sessions at times when Discover is generally quieter throughout the building. Lights and sound levels will be adapted to make these sessions less overwhelming – we’ll also have a specific chillout area outside for those who need a break. Ear defenders will be offered to those who need them (NB these are always available on request at our reception).

383-387 High St, London E15 4QZ Check venue ~28 mins Source: Spektrix (discover)

Dive into a vibrant new story, from the creators of The Rock Family Band, all about self-confidence, self-expression, and finding your own path. With plenty of opportunities to join in, get creative, and draw along, this lively session is perfect for children who love stories, music, and a chance to shine in their own unique way.

Half Moon Theatre
Boxed In
Date TBC
2-5 / family
Book

Boxed In A playful story for anyone who’s ever felt a bit confused, a bit left out, or a bit boxed in. Boxed In is largely non-verbal. This makes it accessible to a wide range of people, including d/Deaf, hard of hearing, BSL users and non-English speakers. Hopeful Monsters

2-5 / family
Book

Hopeful Monsters From simple organisms to complex beasts! A story of evolution told entirely with hands. 43 White Horse Rd, London E1 0ND Check venue ~15 mins Source: Half Moon Theatre
Rapunzel
Date TBC
2-5 / family
Book

Rapunzel Golden-haired Rapunzel is trapped in her lofty tower by a wicked witch, who lops off the locks of her beautiful hair and sells them for a tidy profit. It’s time for Rapunzel to take charge of her own destiny and figure out a way to escape. Read more 25 Jun 2026 - 27 Jun 2026 Ages 3+ 45 minutes Book tickets Thu 25 Jun 10:30 £9 Call Box Office Thu 25 Jun 13:30 £9 Call Box Office Fri 26 Jun 10:30 £9 Sold Out Fri 26 Jun 13:30 £9 Call Box Office Sat 27 Jun 11:00 £9 Book Tickets Sat 27 Jun 14

SPORTS & SWIMMING · 2

Britannia Leisure Centre
Mindfulness
Date TBC
Ages 16+
Book

A relaxing, and self-focused class, experience a range of guided meditations and understand the importance of being ‘still’. Mindfulness is the basic human ability to be fully present, aware of where we are and what we’re doing, and not overly reactive or overwhelmed by what’s going on around us.

Pitfield Street, London, London, N1 5FT Adult £11.1 ~20-35 mins Source: Better OpenActive (CC BY 4.0)
